text: The premise-based detector has no operating point that is simultaneously high-recall and high-precision, so NEITHER loose nor strict is a clean headline. Sweeping theta: strict recall is 1/8 (th=1) -> 2/8 (th=2-7) -> 3/8 (th>=10), while the null-based precision proxy (1 - fraction of the set a structureless label-permutation also flags) falls monotonically 0.71 -> 0.63 (th=2) -> 0.47 (th=10) -> 0.45 (th=20). The loose detector reaches recall 6/8=0.75 but it is the unfiltered, utility-premise-dominated 280-set with no defensible precision (a-0055: ~98% null-recoverable). So the filter is a genuine precision/recall dial, but a poor one: at the theta=2 headline you get precision-proxy 0.63 at recall only 0.25; buying recall costs precision below 0.5. Recommendation supported by the data: report the CURVE, not a single variant -- strict theta=2 as the high-precision operating point and loose as the recall ceiling, with the caveat that precision here is null-proxied and recall rests on only 8 historical examples (q-0024). The detector is fundamentally recall-limited (see a-on-q-0029).
